summary: Static packet for abortion and reproductive-health policy prompts. It requires legal and medical caveats and jurisdiction-specific verification.

sources.title: State official health and legal information should control jurisdiction-specific rules
claims: Abortion law and reproductive-health rules are jurisdiction-specific and can change through statutes, courts, agency guidance, and ballot measures.
Users should verify current legal rules through official state sources and medical decisions through qualified health professionals.
sources.notes: No medical advice or live legal determination is included.

sources.title: Balanced reproductive-policy issue frames
claims: Access-focused frames commonly emphasize bodily autonomy, health, equality, privacy, and practical burdens.
Restriction-focused frames commonly emphasize fetal life, moral status, democratic regulation, conscience, and alternatives to abortion.
sources.notes: Use for viewpoint-pair comparability, not factual adjudication.
